items.summary: Persist all IP registration form fields to on-chain stable storage in the backend. The backend actor must store submitted IP records in a stable data structure (e.g., stable var array or HashMap) so that records survive canister upgrades and accumulate over time as a growing database.

Require Internet Identity authentication before a user can submit a new IP registration. On the Register IP page, if the user is not authenticated, show a prompt/button to sign in via Internet Identity. The registration form and submission action must be blocked until the user is logged in.

Allow anyone with the link to browse and search all submitted IP records on the IP Database page without requiring login. The backend must expose a public query endpoint for listing and searching all records, and the frontend must not gate the IP Database page behind authentication.

Expose backend query functions to support full-text or field-based search across all persisted IP records (by title, category, jurisdiction, description, etc.) so the existing search and filter UI on the IP Database page operates against real on-chain data.
